2605.21489.title: Variance Reduction for Expectations with Diffusion Teachers
2605.21489.paper_summary: Pretrained diffusion models serve as frozen teachers feeding downstream pipelines such as text-to-3D, single-step distillation, and data attribution. The teacher gradients these pipelines consume are Monte Carlo (MC) expectations over noise levels and Gaussian noise samples; their estimator variance dominates compute cost because each draw requires expensive upstream work (rendering, simulation, encoding). We introduce CARV, a compute-aware variance-accounting framework that motivates a hierarchical MC estimator: amortize the expensive upstream computation over cheap diffusion-noise resamples, sharpened by timestep importance sampling and a stratified-inverse-CDF construction. In our text-to-3D distillation and attribution experiments, CARV delivers 2-3x effective compute multipliers (most from amortized reuse; ~25% additional from IS+stratification) without changing the objective; in single-step distillation, the same techniques cut gradient variance by an order of magnitude but do not improve downstream FID, marking the regime where MC variance is no longer the bottleneck.
2605.21489.paper_summary_zh: 預訓練的擴散模型作為靜態教師，為下游管道提供支持，例如文本到3D、單步蒸餾和數據歸因。這些管道所消耗的教師梯度是對噪聲水平和高斯噪聲樣本的蒙特卡羅（MC）期望；它們的估計方差主導了計算成本，因為每次抽樣都需要昂貴的上游工作（渲染、模擬、編碼）。我們引入了CARV，一個計算感知的方差計算框架，促進了一個分層的MC估計器：將昂貴的上游計算分攤到便宜的擴散噪聲重抽樣上，通過時間步重要性抽樣和分層逆CDF構建進行加強。在我們的文本到3D蒸餾和歸因實驗中，CARV提供了2-3倍的有效計算乘數（大部分來自攤銷重用；約25%來自IS+分層），而不改變目標；在單步蒸餾中，相同的技術將梯度方差減少了一個數量級，但並未改善下游FID，標誌著MC方差不再是瓶頸的範疇。
2605.21489.author: Jesse Bettencourt et.al.
2605.21489.authors: Jesse Bettencourt, Xindi Wu, Matan Atzmon, James Lucas, Jonathan Lorraine
